page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r8a.xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs from an AMD EPYC 9T24 processor running at 2.7 GHz, built on the x86_64 architecture. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

In benchmark testing, the ecs.r8a.xlarge demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance, placing in the top 25% of tested instances via stress-ng. However, its multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores fall into the average range. Memory write bandwidth for large blocks is average, but read-only memory bandwidth is relatively weak, landing in the bottom 25%. Similarly, database operations and Redis SET operations show lower-tier performance, indicating potential bottlenecks for highly transactional, read-heavy memory workloads.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.r8a.xlarge offers a dedicated hardware profile with a high resource density of memory relative to compute cores, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require high-speed local storage or extreme read bandwidth. It is well-suited for applications such as caching layers, medium-sized in-memory databases, backend web serving with moderate throughput, and general-purpose enterprise applications requiring a larger memory footprint without the overhead of additional vCPUs.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r8a.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9T24 processor at 2.7 GHz and 32.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under the KVM hypervisor, this instance provides a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or GPU hardware, and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmark results indicate strong single-core CPU performance, though multi-core execution and memory read bandwidth are average to weak. This profile makes the instance qualitatively cost-effective for memory-intensive workloads that are not heavily reliant on high-speed storage or extreme memory read throughput. It is best suited for in-memory caching, moderate database operations, and memory-bound enterprise applications.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and a high memory-to-core ratio for memory-intensive workloads.

32.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
